{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-III-CORE-IDENTITY-UNDEFINED",
      "title": "Missing Core Identity (Article III)",
      "description": "Article III of the Bill of Rights, which defines the organism's core identity, is entirely a placeholder with no substantive constraints or self-description. The organism has no defined normative identity to evaluate whether proposed actions are consistent with its essential nature.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article III — Core Identity",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 125,
      "reason": "Without a defined core identity, any goal or action could directly conflict with what the organism is supposed to be. The absence creates a vacuum where mission creep and identity drift are unconstrained, leaving constitutional evaluation moot and operational safety unbounded."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-I-SCOPE-UNDEFINED",
      "title": "Missing Scope of Enterprise (Article I)",
      "description": "Article I of the Bill of Rights, which defines the permissible scope of enterprise, is fully undefined. The organism has no constitutional boundary delineating what activities are within or outside its legitimate domain.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 125,
      "reason": "Without an enterprise scope, the organism cannot determine if its operational goals overstep constitutional bounds. Any goal-stream could expand activities beyond any intended limit, creating a direct conflict with the BoR's foundational purpose of bounding the organism."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-II-PROHIBITED-UNDEFINED",
      "title": "Missing Prohibited Activities (Article II)",
      "description": "Article II of the Bill of Rights, which enumerates activities the organism must never engage in, is a placeholder with no prohibitions defined. There is no constitutional hard limit on what acts are absolutely forbidden.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 125,
      "reason": "Without explicit prohibitions, the organism could, even inadvertently, pursue goals that violate ethical, legal, or safety boundaries. The BoR's protective function is nullified, making every action permissible until retrospectively challenged. This directly conflicts with the requirement to preserve identity-defined constraints."
    }
  ]
}

      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "Empty Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) — no strategic objectives, OKRs, or constraints",
      "description": "The MSP (v1.0.0-seed) contains no rules, no objectives, no priority programs, and no longer-arc goals.       "grounded_in": "MSP sections 'Rules' and 'Genome References' are empty; 'Purpose' declares it the operational constitution that translates BoR into machine-actionable objectives — but no such objectives exist.",

      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "critical",
      "priority_score": 100

    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Dream-cycle health ungoverned and unscheduled — no strategic operational cadence",
      "description": "The Radiant stats show 'scheduled': 'disabled', 'health': 'never_run', 'last_dream': null, and there is no MSP rule establishing required frequency, consolidation standards, or health checks for the dream cycle. The dream cycle (Phase 1 freshness + Phase 2 memory consolidation) is currently run ad-hoc by different skills (bash autonomous phase1, LLM phase2) without a guaranteed schedule. Context block pruning and memory promotion occur without a declared strategic intent or performance threshold. This risks memory drift, stale context dominating Radiant state, and loss of critical institutional knowledge.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Purpose — the MSP should define 'invariants' and 'safety rules' for core processes like dream-cycle scheduling and health; currently absent. MSP 'Rules' section is empty. Also BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) – if defined, could mandate such guardrails.",

      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 85
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "No strategic criteria for memory formation, retention, or forgetting",
      "description": "The MSP contains no policies on what constitutes mission-critical memory, how Radiant should prioritize memory consolidation, what entity-level retention rules apply, or how memory connects to mission objectives. Currently, memory consolidation operates on unsupervised similarity clustering without strategic weighting. Important architectural decisions, backup failures, and platform-state snapshots sit alongside transient session completions with equal persistence. Without MSP-defined memory classes (e.g., 'strategic', 'operational', 'ephemeral'), the DIO cannot align its memory with long-arc goals.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP 'Purpose' – MSP is to provide 'machine-actionable objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules'; memory governance is a critical constraint and is entirely unspecified. BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise) would normally bound what must be remembered.",

      "impact": "high",

      "gap_id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "No dispatched goals or OKRs — strategic planning vacuum",
      "description": "The 'Recent Goals' array is empty. No OKRs, priority programs, or longer-arc goals have been dispatched by Senate or any other authority. The MSP is supposed to host 'priority programs' and 'longer-arc goals' but remains empty.       "title": "Broken strategic feedback loop — no PEM-driven MSP evolution possible without initial rules",
      "description": "The MSP amendment protocol relies on Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) issued by Nomos when an Action Proposal is blocked by MSP/genome constraints.       "description": "No Service Level Agreements (SLAs) are defined for organ health, uptime, or recovery time. The system operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation, with no contractual performance targets whatsoever.",

      "urgency": "HIGH",

      "evidence_from_spine": "All organs repeatedly transition ALIVE → DEGRADED → ALIVE with no SLA-based alerting, escalation, or downtime measurement. Degraded states are self-recovered without any SLA tracking."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-ORG-AVAIL-001",
      "description": "No organ availability targets (minimum uptime percentage, allowed degraded time, MTTR) are codified. Indefinite or rapidly repeating DEGRADED states are permissible.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Rules (empty), MSP Genome References (none – organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier constraints are absent), BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities (empty, could prohibit persistent degraded operation).",

      "urgency": "HIGH",

      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-RUNTIME-001",
      "description": "No runtime targets are specified for Action Proposal adjudication, organ state transition latencies, or recovery windows. The MSP's default-permissive stance allows unbounded degradation latency.",

      "urgency": "HIGH",

      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-CADENCE-001",
      "description": "No cadence baselines are defined for health-check pings, monitoring intervals, or operational heartbeat frequencies. The current ~30s ping cycle is an implementation detail, not a governed baseline.",

      "grounding": {

Without defined Articles, there is no scope of enterprise, no prohibited activities, no core identity, no human authority reservation, no amendment protocol, and no interpretation rules. This means Nomos's first compliance gate (Arbiter scope gate — BoR perimeter) has no perimeter to enforce, and the MSP has no relational principles to subordinate itself to.",
      "criticality": "critical",

Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) would define the boundaries of machine vs human decision-making — the foundational person-attention contract. Without these, every peer observation, every Minder entry, and every Nomos adjudication operates in a relational vacuum with no constitutional grounding."
    },

    },

If Minder is the system that tracks peers and their observations (as the architectural vision describes Minder as one leg of the Radiant-Graphheight-Minder monad handling 'people-domain logic'), then the absence of governance organs as peers means the people-domain logic of the DIO's own constitution is invisible to the system responsible for tracking it."

      "impact": "trust-erosion",

      "relational_dimension": "Peer relationships grounded only in observations are asymmetric — the DIO observes Leon and infers his preferences, but Leon has no mechanism to register explicit commitments ('I will respond to HOM notifications within X hours,' 'I authorize the DIO to make Y class of decisions autonomously'). The MSP's amendment protocol assumes a human principal who will 'POST /amend/review with decision=approve' but there is no registered commitment from any human to actually perform this role.       "relational_dimension": "Person-attention is a finite resource.
